blockingIssues.description: The reviewed feature commit `6efb253` still does not satisfy its own contract. It solved the compare-suite regression by raising the summary test timeout to 15000ms and did not itself contain the claimed live pool/catalog route wiring or route-level proofs. Current HEAD behavior is correct only because later follow-up features landed the real no-timeout-masking and live-proof work.

Current HEAD fixes the timeout regression with targeted test isolation, but the assigned feature explicitly required the handoff commit to fully contain the claimed implementation. The recorded commit `3aebd7e` is test-only, so scrutiny still cannot validate the broader claimed runtime/live-catalog scope from that feature commit alone.

The implementation behavior now satisfies the live Ethereum token-price resolution contract, but the feature commit `97954aa` bundled unrelated simple-price EUR assertion churn in `tests/app.test.ts`. That scope bleed violates the feature-isolation expectation for scrutiny review even though later stabilization work corrected the test separately.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the onchain-api-worker procedure for follow-up and scrutiny-proof features whose acceptance criteria include commit attribution or proof requirements. Workers should not report success when the recorded `commitId` does not actually contain the implementation/tests the feature claims to deliver.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Two failed reviews in this round remain attribution/scope failures rather than behavior failures: `remove-timeout-masking-from-onchain-compare-coverage` handed off test-only commit `3aebd7e`, and `wire-onchain-pool-endpoints-live` originally used timeout masking plus relied on later commits for the real live-route proof.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance for provider and time-series features to verify ordering and query/result invariants, and to avoid masking regressions by increasing test timeouts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `thegraph-provider-module` originally missed chronological snapshot ordering until later hardening, and `wire-onchain-pool-endpoints-live` addressed a performance regression by extending the compare-suite timeout instead of fixing redundant live fetch cost.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared architecture note says all onchain routes are fixture-backed, which is stale relative to current live DeFiLlama/The Graph behavior.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in prior rounds and outside this validator's additive-update scope

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission/shared-state docs do not record the corrected DeFiLlama `/yields/pools` contract even though later fixes depended on it.
rejectedObservations.reason: knowledge gap is non-blocking and outside this validator's additive-update scope

rejectedObservations.observation: The onchain-api-worker skill still over-prescribes test-first flow for explicit verify-and-commit cleanup sessions.
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking guidance suggestion already captured at the skill level in prior rounds; no direct additive update applied here

orchestratorOverride.justification: All milestone validators are green (`bun run test`, `bun run typecheck`, lint placeholder). The remaining scrutiny blockers are historical feature-commit attribution and scope-isolation issues rather than unresolved runtime or contract defects in current HEAD. Current repository state now includes the later fixes that provide the missing live-catalog proof, no-timeout-masking behavior, chronological snapshot ordering, and generalized live token pricing, and those behaviors are covered by passing tests. Because no user-visible defect remains in the milestone's current implementation, this scrutiny failure is overridden so the mission can proceed to user testing.
historicalAttributionIssues: wire-onchain-pool-endpoints-live original handoff commit 6efb253 did not fully contain the later live proof/runtime fixes
remove-timeout-masking-from-onchain-compare-coverage recorded commit 3aebd7e was narrower than the feature's phrasing
generalize-live-ethereum-token-price-resolution commit 97954aa included unrelated EUR test stabilization churn
